column4: How would you describe your revenue trend over the past 3 years?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: How complete and reliable are your financial records, including balance sheet and tax position?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: What is your underlying profitability relative to your expectations and industry peers?
column9: Profitability carries the single heaviest weight for a profitability objective because it most directly captures the core economic quality of the business.

column4: How well do you understand and manage your pricing strategy and cost structure?
column9: Pricing and cost discipline is the fastest lever for improving underlying earnings.

column4: Do you have recurring or contracted revenue?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: Do you have a documented strategic plan with measurable goals?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: Have you identified and validated clear growth opportunities?
column9: Validated growth opportunities matter most for a growth objective because they show the business knows where expansion will come from.

column4: How actively are you developing new products, services or revenue streams?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: How prepared is your business for a change in ownership, leadership or major capital event?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: Do you understand your business's current market value?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: What is your customer retention rate?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: How concentrated is your revenue, and how direct are your customer relationships?
column9: Revenue concentration is heavily weighted because it affects resilience, bargaining power and valuation across all goals.

column4: How differentiated is your offering from competitors?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: What is the outlook for your industry over the next 5 years?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: Are your core business processes documented and standardised?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: How scalable is your business model?
column9: Scalability is heavily weighted for growth because poor scalability means growth destroys value.

column4: How up-to-date and efficient is your technology stack?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: Do you have meaningful intellectual property (IP)?

column4: How dependent is the business on the owner or other key staff?

column4: Are there any current or pending disputes, litigation or regulatory issues?
column9: Weight reflects how strongly this question influences the selected goal relative to the other four questions in the section.

column4: How well-protected are your key business assets (IP, data, premises)?
